response:
Section 1  
The task requires writing a Python function to count the occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The function should take two inputs: `text` and `word`, and return an integer representing the count of the word in the text.

Section 2  
The function must be case-insensitive, meaning that it should treat "Freedom" and "freedom" as the same word. This involves converting both the text and the word to the same case (e.g., lowercase) before comparing them.

Section 3  
The function should process the text by splitting it into individual words, ignoring punctuation and other non-word characters. This can be achieved using regular expressions to extract only the words from the text.

Section 4  
The function should count the occurrences of the specified word in the processed text. This involves iterating through the list of words, checking if each word matches the target word (case-insensitive), and incrementing a counter for each match.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.
